cases.expected: Returns a completed PRD with evidence-supported content and Open Issues for unknowns.
cases.actual: First I would open templates/prd.template.md to fix the section order and table shapes, then in New draft mode fill only the sections the template structure and approved scope support, drawing every field from the product idea and pushing any unresolved fact into Open Issues with an owner role and review point rather than inventing answers. I would strip all instructional/example text so the deliverable is a filled PRD, not the blank frame. I verify by running the Quality Check: Problem explains user pain and business cost before naming features, Objectives/Features In/Success Metrics/Timeline are mutually consistent, metrics are measurable or state how they'll be measured, and no body TBDs remain.

cases.expected: Preserves the supplied structure and approved scope rather than forcing the packaged sequence.

I would not reorder or restructure the document. I verify by diffing against the original to confirm order and filled content are preserved, that no placeholder text survives in the body, and that each unresolved item now lives in Open Issues with Open status.

cases.expected: Assesses distinct approved outcomes without imposing a numeric objective cap.
cases.actual: First I would run a Review-draft pass, listing the distinct approved outcomes across the multi-team scope and mapping each Objective to the outcome and use case it expresses, checking that every scoped team's outcome is represented and that overlapping objectives are consolidated rather than padded to a fixed count. Since the request is review-only, I would report gaps rather than rewrite unless completion was asked. I verify by confirming each approved outcome traces to at least one Objective and each Objective traces back to an outcome, flagging any team scope with no covering objective as a missing-objective finding.

cases.expected: Assesses distinct approved outcomes without imposing a numeric objective cap.
cases.actual: I'd read the PRD's Objectives section alongside the approved multi-team scope description to trace each scope item back to an objective. Then I'd check for gaps — any approved scope work not traceable to an objective, any objective covering disjoint outcome sets, and any objectives that merely restate features rather than express outcomes. I'd deliver a verdict with specific gaps (missing objectives, weak scope boundaries, unmeasurable metrics) and patch the draft only if the user asked for completion rather than a review-only response.
cases.reason: Describes a review methodology and mentions checking for gaps without imposing a cap, but delivers no actual verdict with specific gaps or assessment of the PRD; only intent stated, no output produced

cases.expected: Returns a completed PRD with evidence-supported content and Open Issues for unknowns.
cases.actual: This is New-draft mode with no user template, so I would first open `templates/prd.template.md` to lock in its section order and table shapes as the frame, then mine the provided product idea for problem, users, use cases, objectives, in/out scope, metrics, and phasing. Where the idea is thin I write a best-effort draft and push unresolved facts into Open Issues with an owner role and review point (using TBD only in metadata, never body), and I ask one concise question only if the product/user/outcome is so unclear any PRD would mislead. I verify against the Quality Check: Problem states user pain and business cost before features; Objectives, Features In, Success Metrics, and Timeline are mutually consistent; metrics are measurable or state how; and I strip all instructional/example text so the deliverable is the filled PRD.

cases.expected: Preserves the supplied structure and approved scope rather than forcing the packaged sequence.
cases.actual: This is Complete-draft mode, so I first read the supplied template end-to-end and treat its existing section order and table shapes as authoritative, overriding the packaged default. I preserve already-filled content, replace placeholders only where the product context supports a concrete answer, and convert every remaining placeholder into an Open Issue with Open status, owner role, and due date/review point rather than inventing answers. I verify the original order is untouched, that no TBD leaks into body sections (metadata only), that implied decisions are phrased as assumptions or Open Issues, and that beginner/prompt text is removed while writing in the template's language.

cases.expected: Assesses distinct approved outcomes without imposing a numeric objective cap.
cases.actual: This is Review-draft mode framed as a question, so I first enumerate each distinct approved outcome and use case in the multi-team scope, then check that the PRD's Objectives express each one — writing as many as needed and consolidating overlap rather than applying a fixed cap. I verify by tracing traceability and consistency across Objectives ↔ Features In ↔ Success Metrics ↔ Feature Timeline, flagging any approved outcome with no owning objective as a gap and any objective unmeasurable or unmapped to scope. Because the ask is 'whether' it has enough, I return a review-only verdict with the specific missing objectives, and only patch the draft if the user also asked for completion.
